div1.entryFree: Anatomize (O. Edd. anathomize, except Lr. III, 6, 80), to dissect for the purpose of examining the interior structure: let them a. Regan, Lr. III, 6, 80. Figuratively == to lay open, to show distinctly: in her the painter had --d time's ruin, Lucr. 1450. should I a. him to thee, As I, 1, 162. the wise man's folly is --d, II, 7, 56. see his company --d, All's IV, 3, 37. my well-known body to a. H4B Ind. All's IV, 3, 37 Don Armado writes annothanize, and uses it in the sense of to explain: which to a. in the vulgar, LLL IV, 1, 69.
